questions.q: What does JSX stand for in React?
Which hook is used to manage state in a functional component?
What is the correct way to pass a prop called 'name' to a component?
Which lifecycle method is called after a component is rendered for the first time in class components?
What does the useEffect hook with an empty dependency array [] do?
options: Runs only once after initial render

questions.q: What is the virtual DOM in React?
options: A direct copy of the DOM
A lightweight copy of the real DOM kept in memory

questions.q: How do you prevent a component from re-rendering when props haven't changed?
What does the key prop do in a list rendered with .map()?
options: Helps React identify which items have changed, are added, or removed

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a valid React hook rule?
options: Only call hooks inside class components
Only call hooks from React functions
Hooks can be called inside loops

questions.q: What is the purpose of useContext hook?
options: To subscribe to React context without using a Consumer

questions.q: What is prop drilling in React?
options: Passing props deep down through components that do not need them
Passing props through multiple nested components that don't need them

questions.q: What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled components?
options: Controlled components have form data handled by React state, uncontrolled rely on DOM

questions.q: What does React.lazy() do?
options: Enables code splitting by dynamically importing components

questions.q: What is the useReducer hook primarily used for?
options: Alternative to useState for simple state
Managing complex state logic with multiple sub-values

questions.q: What is reconciliation in React?
options: Syncing the virtual DOM with the real DOM
Merging multiple state updates into one

questions.q: What does forwardRef do in React?
options: Allows refs to be passed to parent components
Allows parent components to access a child component's DOM node via ref
Creates a copy of a ref
Converts class refs to functional refs

questions.q: Which of the following correctly describes React Fragments?
options: They are used for code splitting
They are only available in class components

questions.q: What is the purpose of the useCallback hook?
options: To memoize a function itself so it doesn't get recreated on every render

questions.q: When does the cleanup function in useEffect run?
options: Before the component re-runs the effect and before it unmounts

questions.q: What is the difference between useMemo and useCallback?
options: useMemo memoizes values; useCallback memoizes functions
useMemo is for async operations, useCallback is synchronous
useCallback is deprecated in favor of useMemo

questions.q: What is an Error Boundary in React?
options: A class component that catches JavaScript errors anywhere in the child component tree
A hook for handling runtime errors

questions.q: What does the Suspense component do?
options: Lets you specify a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components

questions.q: In React, what is a Higher-Order Component (HOC)?
options: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component
A component with higher priority in rendering

questions.q: What does the second argument to useState() represent?
options: A function to track dependency array changes
A function to update the state

questions.q: What is the correct way to update state based on the previous state?
Which React hook would you use to access a DOM element directly?
What is the purpose of React.StrictMode?
options: Highlights potential problems by checking warnings
Highlights potential problems in an application by activating additional warnings

questions.q: What happens when you call setState() in React class components?
options: State is scheduled for an update and re-render may be batched
All child components are destroyed and recreated
A new component instance is created

questions.q: What is the render prop pattern in React?
options: Passing JSX content via rendering props
Sharing code using a prop whose value is a function that returns JSX
Rendering components with a specific priority

questions.q: What is the purpose of the children prop in React?
options: Allows child components to render HTML
It allows components to pass JSX content between the opening and closing tags
It restricts what types of children can be rendered
It is used for CSS child selectors

questions.q: What is batching in React?
options: Grouping multiple state updates into a single re-render for performance

questions.q: In React Router v6, which component defines the routes?
What is the difference between state and props?
options: State is immutable, props are mutable
State is managed within a component, props are passed from parent to child
State is for class components only, props are for functional components

questions.q: What does dangerouslySetInnerHTML do in React?
options: Allows rendering raw HTML, bypassing React's DOM diffing (XSS risk)

questions.q: How do you optimize performance when a child component receives the same props?
options: Use useEffect with an empty array

questions.q: What is the significance of the displayName property on a component?
options: It is used for component styles
It helps with debugging by showing a friendly name in React DevTools
It is required for every component
It renders a label above the component

questions.q: Which of the following is a correct way to conditionally render a component?
What is Server-Side Rendering (SSR) in the context of React?
options: Executing React code on the server side
Generating HTML on the server and sending it to the client for faster initial load
Storing React state on the server

questions.q: What is the purpose of the useImperativeHandle hook?
options: To customize the ref instance value exposed when using forwardRef

questions.q: What is the React Context API primarily used for?
options: Passing data through the component tree without prop drilling

questions.q: What is a pure component in React?
options: A component that only renders when its props or state change (shallow comparison)
A component with no side effects

questions.q: What is the purpose of React.createPortal()?
options: Renders children into a separate DOM node hierarchy
Renders children into a DOM node outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy

questions.q: What is the correct way to update an object in state immutably?
What does useLayoutEffect differ from useEffect?
options: useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutations but before the browser paints
useLayoutEffect is for CSS calculations only

questions.q: What is hydration in React?
options: The process of attaching event listeners to server-rendered HTML on the client
Filling empty props with default values

questions.q: How does React handle events compared to vanilla JavaScript?
options: React events are identical to browser events
React uses synthetic events that wrap native events for cross-browser consistency

questions.q: What is the significance of React's one-way data flow?
options: Props can flow two ways in strict mode
Data flows from parent to child through props, making state predictable
Components can only have one prop
State updates are one-directional from right to left

questions.q: When would you use useRef over useState?
options: When you want to force re-render
When you need to persist a mutable value without causing re-renders
When you need to share state between components
When you are managing form inputs

questions.q: What is the purpose of getDerivedStateFromProps?
options: To derive parent state from child props
To update state based on changes in props before rendering

questions.q: What does React's concurrent mode enable?
options: Interruptible rendering so React can pause, resume, or abandon work to keep the UI responsive
Running multiple React versions side by side
